sections.paragraph:
τετραάρχης, ου, ὁ (some edd. spell it τετράρχης; on this s. B-D-F §124; W-S.§5, 24b; Mlt.-H. 63 al.) a petty prince dependent on Rome and with rank and authority lower than those of a king, tetrarch (Strabo, Joseph., ins: s. the reff. in Schürer I 333–35 n. 12. Also Plut., Anton. 942 [56, 7]; 943 [58, 11]; Polyaenus 8, 39), orig., ruler of the fourth part of a region (Strabo 12, 5, 1 [567]); later, when the orig. sense was wholly lost (Appian, Mithrid. 46 §178; 58 §236 there are more than four Galatian tetrarchs), title of a petty prince who ruled by courtesy of Rome. In our lit. Herod Antipas is given this title (as well as in OGI 416, 3; 417, 4; Jos., Ant. 17, 188; 18, 102; 109; 122) Mt 14:1; Lk 3:19; 9:7; Ac 13:1; ISm 1:2.—BNiese, RhM n.s. 38, 1883, 583ff; Pauly-W. 2, IV 1089–97; Kl. Pauly V 632f; BHHW III 1956f.—DELG s.v. ἀρχω C. M-M.

edd. edd. = editions, editors

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt. Mlt. = JMoulton; with page number alone specified, this abbr. refers to A Grammar of NT Greek I: Prolegomena—List 6

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.
  2. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

orig. orig. = original(ly)

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

RhM RhM = Rheinisches Museum für Philologie—List 6

n.s. n.s. = new series (also used to render Neue Folge)

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1000
